name: Castellot de Castellví — circular by the Casas Noves de la Riera
name_original: Castellot de Castellví — circular por las Casas Noves de la Riera
region_intro: Jogging around Castellví De La Marca offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by expansive vineyards and dense forests. The region features hills like El Castellot, providing panoramic views of the Penedès plain. A Mediterranean climate supports outdoor activities throughout the year, with varied terrain including riverine environments and well-maintained paths. Best jogging routes around Castellví De La Marca The most popular jogging route is Castellot de Castellví — circular by the Casas Noves de la Riera, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path around the notable Castellot hill. Another top favourite among local runners is Sant Sadurní de Castellví – El Castellot loop from les Cases Noves de Cal Marques, a difficult 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. This route provides an extended loop with significant elevation gain, traversing varied landscapes. Local runners also love the Riera de Marmellar – El Castellot loop from Castellví de la Marca, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through riverine areas and towards El Castellot, often completed in about 42 minutes. Jogging around Castellví De La Marca is defined by rolling vineyards, lush forests, and scenic hills, offering routes for various ability levels from moderate to difficult. The routes in Castellví De La Marca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,400 runners have used komoot to explore Castellví De La Marca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Castellví de la Marca?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;10 dedicated jogging routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Castellví de la Marca, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Castellví de la Marca?
A: The running trails in Castellví de la Marca are characterized by &lt;em&gt;rolling vineyards&lt;/em&gt;, &lt;em&gt;lush forests&lt;/em&gt;, and &lt;em&gt;scenic hills&lt;/em&gt;. You'll find varied terrain, including riverine environments and well-maintained paths, providing a mix of surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Castellví de la Marca?
A: While many routes in the area are considered more challenging, there is one moderate route available: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17516154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Marmellar – El Castellot loop from Castellví de la Marca&lt;/a&gt;. This 3.9-mile (6.3 km) trail offers a less strenuous option through riverine areas towards El Castellot.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints in Castellví de la Marca?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in the region offer breathtaking panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1130755411/castellot-de-castellvi-circular-by-the-casas-noves-de-la-riera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellot de Castellví — circular by the Casas Noves de la Riera&lt;/a&gt; route takes you around El Castellot, a natural viewpoint at 465 meters, providing exceptional vistas of the Penedès plain and surrounding mountain ranges. Puig de l'Àliga also offers incredible views.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Castellví de la Marca?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Castellví de la Marca are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17518180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Sadurní de Castellví – El Castellot loop from les Cases Noves de Cal Marques&lt;/a&gt;, which provides an extended loop through varied landscapes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Castellví de la Marca?
A: The running routes in Castellví de la Marca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.8 stars from over 20 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the vineyards and forests, and the rewarding panoramic views from elevated points like El Castellot.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by historical sites or natural attractions?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with rich history. You can find routes that pass by significant landmarks such as the medieval Castell de la Marca or Romanesque churches. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10431316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castellot – Ermita de Sant Miquel Ruins loop from Castellví de la Marca&lt;/a&gt; takes you past historical ruins. Additionally, you might encounter natural features like the Foix River or various caves.

Q: What are some notable attractions I might see near the running routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, the area offers several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself, offering scenic spots for a break.

Q: Is there a dedicated sports circuit for running in Castellví de la Marca?
A: Yes, the municipality features the &lt;strong&gt;Anella Verda (Green Ring)&lt;/strong&gt;, a dedicated sports circuit equipped with biosaludable machines at five different points. This route is clearly signposted with blue markers and is ideal for combining your run with other exercises.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Castellví de la Marca?
A: Thanks to its Mediterranean climate, Castellví de la Marca is suitable for outdoor activities, including jogging, &lt;strong&gt;throughout the year&lt;/strong&gt;. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer mornings or evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to run comfortably.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running options for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19845696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Pascol Tower – El Castellot loop from Castellví de la Marca&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.6-mile (20.3 km) route with significant elevation gain, providing a rigorous workout amidst stunning scenery.

Q: Are there any running paths that follow ancient or historical trails?
A: Yes, Castellví de la Marca is traversed by sections of historical paths. A part of the ancient &lt;strong&gt;Camí Ramader de Marina&lt;/strong&gt;, a transhumance route, passes through the area. Additionally, a branch of the &lt;strong&gt;Camí de Sant Jaume (St. James Way)&lt;/strong&gt;, specifically from Vilafranca to Sant Jaume dels Domenys, also runs through the municipality, offering a historical and well-trodden path for longer runs.
tips: The riera de Marmellar is one of the streams that surrounds the Castellot de Castellví. Deep valleys between rapidly rising mountains.

Located on the hill of the same name, at an altitude of 468 meters, on the Marmellar stream and dominating the entire extension of the Penedès plain, the Castellot is one of the most recognizable surveillance and defense structures of the medieval period.

This small fortress had the mission of serving as a lookout and defense of the territory in the event of an attack by the Saracens. Due to its small size, it never had a castle structure, it was just a watchtower and defense tower, with a small attached construction and next to it the primitive chapel of Sant Miquel.

The complex is dated between the end of the 10th century and the beginning of the 11th century, with some modifications made in the 13th century, before being abandoned and becoming a temporary refuge for shepherds, vagabonds and bandits. A second phase of restoration was completed in March 2021, mainly focused on the watchtower, the upper part of which can now be accessed via an internal staircase.

This church is sandwiched between trees and the Castellví de la Marca cemetery and can only be seen clearly from the path that comes from Sant Jaume dels Domenys. In order to see its base you have to go to one of the entrances of the cemetery.
